Sanguine Biosciences is accelerating personalized medicine research by empowering patients with their healthcare data. They are seeking an AI Engineer I to join their Revenue Operations team to help build AI-powered automation tools, working closely with a Senior Salesforce Solutions Architect.

Responsibilities
Build, test, and deploy AI agents and LLM-powered automation workflows
Work hands-on with Python to analyze data, experiment with new natural language model architectures, perform prompt engineering on LLMs, benchmark and monitor model accuracy, deploy models into production for real-time inferencing to support various use cases
Develop and maintain prompt engineering strategies for AI-powered pipelines, optimizing for reliability, accuracy, and performance
Integrate AI outputs with Salesforce and other enterprise systems by structuring and routing model responses into the correct records, objects, and workflows
Build PDF and document extraction pipelines using LLM models to parse clinical and operational documents and feed structured data downstream
Assist in designing and evaluating Salesforce Agentforce and Einstein AI use cases in collaboration with the Salesforce Architect
Work hands-on with node-based automation. Handle error triggers, wait nodes, and pass JSON data between different API services
Develop and maintain automation workflows connecting AI services, Salesforce, and other operational platforms
Write clean, well-documented Python scripts and API integrations to extend AI agent capabilities and support data transformation pipelines
Support the monitoring and error handling of AI-powered workflows, troubleshooting failures and improving pipeline resilience
Assist in implementing webhook-based and event-driven architectures to trigger AI pipelines from operational system activity
Partner with Operations, Sales, and Finance teams to identify and scope new AI agent use cases that drive efficiency and reduce manual effort
Stay current with rapidly evolving AI tooling, LLM capabilities, and agentic frameworks, bringing new ideas and approaches to the team
Contribute to Sanguine's AI-first development philosophy by leveraging AI tooling for code generation, documentation, and testing under established quality standards
Document AI agent architectures, prompt designs, integration patterns, and deployment procedures in clear, implementation-ready runbooks
Write and maintain test cases for AI workflows to validate output quality, catch regressions, and ensure model behavior meets business requirements
Participate in code reviews and contribute to team development standards under the guidance of the Senior Salesforce Architect
Track and report on AI agent performance metrics and operational ROI, supporting data-driven decision-making for future investments
Work alongside the Senior Salesforce Solutions Architect and Technology Platforms Director on a day-to-day basis, learning enterprise Salesforce development patterns while contributing AI

Qualification
check

Workflow Automation

Salesforce

Required
Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, Data Science, or a related field (recent graduates encouraged to apply)
Demonstrated experience building with LLMs and AI APIs (e.g., Anthropic Claude, OpenAI, Gemini) through coursework, personal projects, internships, or open-source work
Proficiency in Python, including working with REST APIs, JSON data structures, and automation scripting
Familiarity with workflow automation tools such as N8N, Zapier, Make, or similar platforms
Understanding of prompt engineering principles and practical experience constructing prompts for complex, multi-step tasks
Ability to write clean, readable, and well-documented code suitable for production environments
Strong problem-solving skills and intellectual curiosity - comfortable researching unfamiliar tools and working through ambiguity independently
Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to explain technical concepts to non-technical stakeholders
Preferred
Experience building AI agent workflows with tool use, multi-step reasoning, or retrieval-augmented generation (RAG)
Exposure to Salesforce development (LWC, Apex, Flow, or REST/SOAP APIs)
Familiarity with version control (Git) and basic CI/CD concepts
Experience with JavaScript or Node.js
Knowledge of healthcare, life sciences, or clinical research data environments
Prior internship or project experience integrating AI pipelines with enterprise SaaS platforms (CRM, ERP, LIMS, etc)
